openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon/adapt.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing planaddon.Repository — the persistence layer for plan-to-addon assignment records. Owns all SQL queries, soft-delete logic, and eager-loading of related plan and addon entities via the Tx/WithTx/Self triad required by entutils.TransactingRepo.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping every method
patterns.description: Every exported adapter method wraps its body in entutils.TransactingRepo[T, *adapter](ctx, a, fn). The fn closure receives a tx-rebound *adapter; never call a.db directly outside this closure.

patterns.name: Tx/WithTx/Self triad
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx (via a.db.HijackTx + entutils.NewTxDriver), WithTx (via entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self (returns a). All three are required for TransactingRepo to rebind to caller-supplied transactions.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Post-create/update refetch with eager loads
patterns.description: After Save() or Update(), re-query with WithPlan(PlanEagerLoadPhasesWithRateCardsWithFeaturesFn).WithAddon(AddonEagerLoadRateCardsWithFeaturesFn) because Ent Save() does not populate edges.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via SetDeletedAt + clock.Now()
patterns.description: Delete sets DeletedAt to clock.Now().UTC() via UpdateOneID, never hard-deletes. List/Get queries add planaddondb.DeletedAtIsNil() unless IncludeDeleted is set.

patterns.name: Typed NotFoundError wrapping
patterns.description: entdb.IsNotFound(err) must be caught and re-wrapped as planaddon.NewNotFoundError(NotFoundErrorParams{...}) so callers receive a domain-typed error instead of a raw Ent error.
patterns.example: if entdb.IsNotFound(err) { return nil, planaddon.NewNotFoundError(planaddon.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: params.Namespace, ID: params.ID}) }

patterns.name: Shared package-level eager-load vars
patterns.description: PlanEagerLoadPhasesWithRateCardsWithFeaturesFn and AddonEagerLoadRateCardsWithFeaturesFn are package-level vars reused across all queries to keep edge loading consistent across methods.

patterns.name: Validated Config constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates required fields (Client, Logger) via Config.Validate() — returns an error rather than panicking if any field is nil. var _ models.Validator = (*Config)(nil) enforces the interface at compile time.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config, constructor New, and the adapter struct with Tx/WithTx/Self. No query logic lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must implement the full TxCreator+TxUser triad exactly or TransactingRepo will fail to rebind. var _ planaddon.Repository = (*adapter)(nil) compile-check must pass.

key_file_guides.role: All CRUD method implementations. Each method closure is passed to entutils.TransactingRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Avoid calling a.db directly outside a TransactingRepo closure — always use the closure's rebound *adapter. For DeletePlanAddon, get-then-soft-delete pattern must stay consistent.

key_file_guides.role: FromPlanAddonRow converts entdb.PlanAddon to planaddon.PlanAddon, delegating plan/addon edge mapping to planadapter.FromPlanRow and addonadapter.FromAddonRow.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Edges are optional — always guard with `if a.Edges.Plan != nil` and `if a.Edges.Addon != nil` before dereferencing.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests against a real Postgres schema using pctestutils.NewTestEnv. Tests cover Create/Get/List/Update/Delete via env.PlanAddonRepository.
key_file_guides.watch_for: env.DBSchemaMigrate(t) must be called before any query. Tests use context.Background() (pre-t.Context() era) — keep consistent with existing style.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly in a method body without wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses ctx-bound transaction.
Hard-deleting rows via Delete() instead of soft-deleting via SetDeletedAt.
Returning raw entdb.IsNotFound errors — always wrap in planaddon.NewNotFoundError.
Adding business-logic validation (plan status checks, conflict detection) inside the adapter — those belong in the service layer.
Skipping eager-load function vars when re-fetching after create/update — callers expect fully populated Plan and Addon edges.

decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate implicitly in ctx; using the raw *entdb.Client falls off a caller-supplied transaction and causes partial writes under concurrency.

decisions.decision: Post-create refetch instead of using Save() return value
decisions.rationale: Ent's Save() does not populate relation edges; a separate query with WithPlan/WithAddon is the only way to return a fully hydrated domain object.
Plan-addon assignments must remain queryable (with IncludeDeleted) for audit and reconciliation after removal.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new mutation method following the established adapter pattern

openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon/httpd.purpose: HTTP handler layer for plan-addon assignment CRUD endpoints, bridging the v1 REST API (api.PlanAddon*) to planaddon.Service via the httptransport.HandlerWithArgs generic pattern. Owns request decoding, response mapping, and error encoding only — no business logic.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for every endpoint
patterns.description: Each handler method returns a typed HandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params] constructed inline with a decoder closure, an operation closure, and a ResponseEncoder. Never implement ServeHTTP directly.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
patterns.description: All decoders call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) which delegates to h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx). Never read namespace from URL params or request headers directly.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: ValidationErrorEncoder per operation
patterns.description: Every operation appends httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ValidationErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ResourceKindAddon)) so domain validation errors are encoded as RFC 7807 Problem Details.

patterns.name: Local type aliases in planaddon.go
patterns.description: Each endpoint declares local type aliases (e.g. CreatePlanAddonRequest = planaddon.CreatePlanAddonInput) next to the handler type declaration for self-documentation.
patterns.example: type (CreatePlanAddonRequest = planaddon.CreatePlanAddonInput; CreatePlanAddonResponse = api.PlanAddon; CreatePlanAddonHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...])

patterns.name: Handler interface embeds sub-interfaces
patterns.description: Handler interface embeds PlanAddonHandler, which declares one factory method per endpoint returning typed handler values. var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) enforces compile-time interface satisfaction.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { PlanAddonHandler }; type PlanAddonHandler interface { ListPlanAddons() ListPlanAddonsHandler; ... }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler and PlanAddonHandler interfaces, handler struct, New constructor, and resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new endpoint methods must appear in both the PlanAddonHandler interface and the handler struct for var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) to pass. resolveNamespace returns 500 (not 400) on missing namespace.

key_file_guides.role: One function per HTTP operation; each builds and returns a HandlerWithArgs using inline decoder/operation closures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Status codes must match API spec: 200 for list/get/update, 201 for create, 204 (EmptyResponseEncoder) for delete. Do not add business logic here.

key_file_guides.role: FromPlanAddon converts domain planaddon.PlanAddon to api.PlanAddon. AsCreatePlanAddonRequest/AsUpdatePlanAddonRequest convert API body types to domain input types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FromPlanAddon calls a.AsProductCatalogPlanAddon().ValidationErrors() — validation issues must always be propagated to the response ValidationErrors field, never swallowed.

anti_patterns: Implementing net/http.Handler directly on handler methods — always use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs.
Resolving namespace from URL path parameters instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx).
Omitting WithErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ValidationErrorEncoder(...)) from an operation's options — validation errors will not serialize correctly.
Adding business logic (plan/addon status checks, conflict detection) in decoder or mapping layer — decoders parse only.
Returning raw domain errors without letting the error encoder chain handle them.

decisions.decision: httptransport.HandlerWithArgs generic pattern for all operations
decisions.rationale: Separates HTTP concerns (decode, encode, error handling) from business logic and enables consistent middleware chaining (OTel, error encoding) across all endpoints without boilerplate.

decisions.decision: Namespace injected via NamespaceDecoder rather than path/query param
decisions.rationale: Self-hosted deployments use a static default namespace; the decoder abstraction allows both self-hosted (static) and multi-tenant (header/JWT) resolvers without changing handler code.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new read-only endpoint following the httpdriver pattern

openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon/servi.purpose: Business-logic layer implementing planaddon.Service — validates inputs, enforces plan/addon state constraints (status, deleted checks), delegates persistence to planaddon.Repository, and publishes domain events after every mutation. No Ent or SQL imports allowed here.

patterns.name: transaction.Run / RunWithNoValue for write operations
patterns.description: Mutating methods (CreatePlanAddon, DeletePlanAddon, UpdatePlanAddon) wrap their fn closure in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, fn) or transaction.RunWithNoValue to ensure atomicity across adapter calls and event publishing.

patterns.name: Validate params first, then cross-entity checks
patterns.description: Every method calls params.Validate() immediately on entry, then fetches related entities (plan, addon) to enforce business rules (status, deleted state) before calling the adapter.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid ...: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Domain-specific errors wrapped as models.Generic* errors
patterns.description: plan.NotFoundError, addon.NotFoundError, planaddon.NotFoundError are caught via errors.As and re-wrapped as models.NewGenericNotFoundError. Validation failures use models.NewGenericValidationError. Duplicates use models.NewGenericConflictError.
patterns.example: if notFound := &(plan.NotFoundError{}); errors.As(err, &notFound) { return nil, models.NewGenericNotFoundError(err) }

patterns.name: Publish domain event after every successful mutation
patterns.description: After successful create/update/delete, publish a typed event (planaddon.NewPlanAddonCreateEvent, UpdateEvent, DeleteEvent) via s.publisher inside the transaction closure — failure rolls back the DB change.
patterns.example: event := planaddon.NewPlanAddonCreateEvent(ctx, planAddon); if err = s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Config struct with required-field nil-checks in New()
patterns.description: service.New(Config) checks Adapter, Plan, Addon, Logger, Publisher for nil and returns an error, enforcing complete wiring at construction time. var _ planaddon.Service = (*service)(nil) enforces compile-time interface satisfaction.
patterns.example: if config.Adapter == nil { return nil, errors.New("add-on assignment adapter is required") }

patterns.name: Patch-field merge rules mirrored and documented between service and adapter
patterns.description: UpdatePlanAddon builds a merged draft PlanAddon for Validate() before calling the adapter. Merge rules (MaxQuantity always replaced via SetOrClearMaxQuantity; FromPlanPhase/Metadata/Annotations only written when non-nil) must exactly match the adapter's behaviour.
patterns.example: // MaxQuantity is always replaced via SetOrClearMaxQuantity, so nil clears the column — use params.MaxQuantity as-is.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config, New constructor, and service struct. No method logic lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: var _ planaddon.Service = (*service)(nil) compile-check — add new interface methods here before implementing them in planaddon.go.

key_file_guides.role: All planaddon.Service method implementations. Read methods call fn(ctx) directly; write methods use transaction.Run / RunWithNoValue.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DeletePlanAddon re-fetches the record after deletion to get the DeletedAt timestamp for the event payload — do not skip this refetch. Always publish the event inside the transaction closure.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests exercising the full service+adapter+DB stack via pctestutils.NewTestEnv. Covers invalid-state transitions (e.g. published plan rejects new plan-addon).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests create meters, features, plans, and addons as prerequisites — reuse pctestutils helpers rather than duplicating fixture code.

anti_patterns: Calling s.adapter methods outside a transaction.Run closure for write paths — risks partial writes if publisher.Publish fails.
Returning adapter-level errors (planaddon.NotFoundError) directly without wrapping in models.Generic* — breaks the HTTP error encoder chain.
Checking plan/addon status inside the adapter — status validation belongs exclusively in the service layer.
Omitting event publishing after a successful mutation — downstream systems depend on these events for sync.
Using context.Background() instead of the passed ctx — breaks tracing and cancellation.

decisions.rationale: Create/Update/Delete each call the adapter multiple times (get-then-mutate pattern) and publish an event; all must be atomic so a publish failure rolls back the DB change.

decisions.decision: Cross-entity validation (plan status, addon status) in the service not the adapter
decisions.rationale: Adapters are pure persistence; business rules about which states allow assignment creation/deletion must be in the service to stay testable without a DB.

decisions.decision: Patch-field merge logic mirrored and documented between service and adapter
decisions.rationale: UpdatePlanAddon builds a draft merged state for Validate() before calling the adapter; the merge rules must exactly match the adapter's SetOrClear/conditional-set behaviour to avoid silent divergence.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new write service method with transaction, validation, and event publishing
